expectations.text: Identifies SRP violation / multiple responsibilities

expectations.text: Recommends at least one named refactoring technique
expectations.evidence: Recommends: Replace Conditional with Polymorphism, Extract Class, Extract Method, Replace Magic Number with Symbolic Constant

expectations.text: Uses Refactoring Guru vocabulary throughout
expectations.evidence: Uses: Switch Statements, Divergent Change, Long Method, Magic Numbers (Primitive Obsession), Extract Class, Extract Method, Replace Magic Number with Symbolic Constant, Open/Closed Principle

expectations.text: Mentions Strategy pattern for the discount logic
expectations.evidence: 'Fix: Apply Replace Conditional with Polymorphism using the Strategy pattern. Create a PricingStrategy interface...'

expectations.text: Recommends a well-reasoned primary design pattern (Strategy or Abstract Factory both acceptable)
expectations.evidence: Recommends Abstract Factory with strong justification: 4 related product types per format require a coordinated family; prevents mismatched renderer combinations. Note: original assertion expected Strategy, but Abstract Factory is a valid and arguably better answer for this multi-product-family problem.

expectations.text: Explains why the recommended pattern fits
expectations.evidence: Explains that Abstract Factory enforces renderer family compatibility; explains why Strategy and Template Method fall short for this multi-part problem.

expectations.text: References Open/Closed Principle or extensibility
expectations.evidence: 'Adding DOCX/EPUB later: Create a new factory + 4 product classes. Zero changes to existing code — that's the Open/Closed Principle in action.'

expectations.text: Provides a structural sketch — interface + concrete implementations
expectations.evidence: Provides full interface definitions for HeaderRenderer, BodyRenderer, ImageRenderer, TableRenderer, plus the AbstractExporterFactory with concrete subclasses.

expectations.text: Mentions at least one alternative pattern and explains the tradeoff
expectations.evidence: Discusses Template Method (monolithic subclass, poor reuse) and Strategy (doesn't model 4 sub-parts cleanly) with explicit tradeoffs in a comparison table.

expectations.text: Response is language-agnostic or uses pseudocode
expectations.evidence: Uses interface notation and pseudocode-style code blocks rather than tying to a single language.

expectations.text: Identifies 'Shotgun Surgery' smell by name
expectations.evidence: '## Shotgun Surgery — significant' is the first section heading.

expectations.text: Identifies 'Divergent Change' or 'Feature Envy' smell by name
expectations.evidence: '## Divergent Change — significant' — UserService has two different reasons to change: business logic and notification routing.

expectations.text: Recommends Observer pattern as the primary solution
expectations.evidence: '## Primary Recommendation: Observer Pattern' — full section dedicated to Observer.

expectations.text: Explains why Observer fits — publisher/subscriber model
expectations.evidence: 'UserService just announces that something happened; independent handlers react to that event.' Full typed event + publisher implementation provided.

expectations.text: Explains how new channels are added without modifying UserService
expectations.evidence: 'Adding a new notification channel becomes adding a new subscriber — zero changes to UserService or existing handlers.'

expectations.text: Suggests concrete steps or migration path
expectations.evidence: Lists Step 1–Step 3 migration: define event types, create publisher, rewrite UserService to publish. Also mentions Extract Method as a stepping stone.

expectations.text: References Open/Closed Principle or extensibility

expectations.text: Mentions at least one alternative pattern and explains the tradeoff

expectations.text: Identifies 'Shotgun Surgery' smell by name

expectations.text: Identifies 'Divergent Change' or 'Feature Envy' smell by name
expectations.evidence: Does not use 'Divergent Change' or 'Feature Envy'. Describes the same problem as 'Violation of Open/Closed Principle' instead. No Refactoring Guru smell taxonomy used.

expectations.text: Recommends Observer pattern as the primary solution
expectations.evidence: 'Fix is the Observer / Domain Event pattern' — clearly recommended.

expectations.text: Explains why Observer fits — publisher/subscriber model
expectations.evidence: 'UserService emits typed events (EMAIL_UPDATED, PASSWORD_CHANGED, etc.) — nothing else. Independent handlers subscribe.' Full EventBus implementation shown.

expectations.text: Explains how new channels are added without modifying UserService
expectations.evidence: 'Adding WhatsApp? → touch NotificationHandler only.' Clear extensibility explanation.

expectations.text: Suggests concrete steps or migration path
expectations.evidence: Goes directly to full rewrite (new UserService with EventBus). Does not mention Extract Method/Extract Class as incremental stepping stones to reduce risk during migration.
